intent:
Mother and the narrator are drinking evening tea in the kitchen; the window is already open. A small black bird flies in, perches on a chair, and sings. Mother asks where it came from; the bird flies back out the same window; mother answers her own question by naming it a garden friend. Anchor is the bird: it enters (s2), perches and calls (s3), is questioned (s4), departs (s5), and is named (s6). Reinforces W00108 (鳥), W00109 (飛ぶ), W00110 (どこ) under R1; reinforces grammar N5_doko_where (intro story 19) inside the mother's question. Introduces grammar N5_kara_because in s2 (window open → bird entered) — a clause-level causal connector that opens up reasoned narration without leaving N5. Mints 開く (intransitive open, balances 開ける at s1 to depict a state), 黒い (color of bird), 鳴く (bird call), 庭 (garden, mother's interpretive answer in the closer). 4 mints; min 3, max 5; well within budget.

PREMISE CONTRACT (story 20)
  one_sentence_event:    A small black bird flies into the kitchen through the open window, sings on a chair, and the mother — opening the window wider so it can leave — names it a garden friend.
  change_of_state:       at s0 mother and narrator are alone with tea; at closer the bird has come, sung, departed, and been named — narrator's understanding of the visitor changes from "intruder" to "neighbor."
  scene_novelty_claim:   I am NOT writing an entryway / park / station story. Scene is the kitchen at dusk.
  anchor_novelty_claim:  Anchor is 鳥. Last appeared in story 19 as the question-object (where did the birds go); here it is the agent that enters and leaves the human space.
  closer_shape_claim:    Closer shape: dialogue line — quote-TO-mother-WA-verb. NOT a noun-pile, NOT a 「Nは Adj です」 mirror, NOT a sky-question mirror of story 19's closer.
  why_not_filler:        母 anchors the dialogue and acts (opens window, names bird) — without her the bird's entry is unwitnessed. 私 holds the narrator POV (drinks tea with mother in s0). 鳥 is the agent of every state change. 椅子 is the perch that lets the bird sing within reach. 窓 is the threshold the bird crosses twice. お茶 is the quiet baseline the bird disrupts.
  obligations_absorbed:  must-reinforce W00108/W00109/W00110 absorbed naturally — bird is the protagonist; どこ is the mother's first reaction; 飛ぶ is how the bird enters/lands/leaves. must-introduce N5_kara_because absorbed in s2 (window-open → bird-entered) — the most natural causal pairing on the page.

FORBIDDEN THIS STORY:
  scene_class    (last 3): entryway, park, station
  anchor_object  (last 5): ノート, パン, 窓, 電車, 靴
  closer_shape   (last 3): noun-GA-noun-NI-noun-KARA-noun, noun-TO-noun-WA-noun-WO-noun-noun-E-noun, noun-WA-noun-WO-noun-noun-WA-noun-E-noun-P:か-TO-noun
  opening_seqs   (last 3): 今日/私/家, 次/朝/玄関, 次/駅/二人

sentences.jp: 夕方、母と私は台所でお茶を飲みました。
sentences.en: In the evening, my mother and I drank tea in the kitchen.

sentences.jp: 台所の窓は開いていました。
窓が開いていたから、小さい黒い鳥が台所に飛んで来ました。
sentences.en: Because the window was open, a small black bird flew into the kitchen.

sentences.jp: 鳥は椅子に止まって、鳴きました。
sentences.en: The bird perched on a chair and sang.

sentences.jp: 母は「この鳥はどこから来ましたか」と言いました。
sentences.en: Mother said, "Where did this bird come from?"

sentences.jp: 鳥は窓から飛んで行きました。
sentences.en: The bird flew away through the window.

sentences.jp: 「庭の友達です」と母が答えました。
sentences.en: "A friend from the garden," mother answered.
